stem: An engineer takes 15 measurements of PM2.5 concentration using an air quality sensor. The measurements have a mean of 72.2 µg/m³ and a standard deviation of 10.1 µg/m³. However, calibration tests reveal that an air quality sensor systematically underestimates the true value by between 2.3% and 6.3%. Estimate the true PM2.5 concentration and provide a confidence interval that accounts for both the sampling variability and the uncertain systematic bias.

ground_truth.answer: Raw mean: 72.2 µg/m³. After correcting for the 4.3% bias: ≈ 75.4 µg/m³. Full interval accounting for bias uncertainty (2.3%-6.3%) and sampling variability: [68.7, 82.3]. Point estimate: 75.4 µg/m³.

common_failure_modes: Using the raw mean without correcting for the systematic bias
Correcting for bias at a single value instead of treating it as a range
Not combining the sampling variability with the bias uncertainty
Treating the systematic bias as random noise instead of a directional shift
